summary: Docker-based reproduction confirms unbounded query limits cause server resource exhaustion and unresponsiveness
expected_security_impact: Server unresponsive or OOM when querying with unbounded limit parameter
actual_observed_result: Vulnerability confirmed: maxLimit defaults to undefined allowing unlimited query results. Attack scenario validated showing unauthenticated queries with limit=1000000 cause memory exhaustion and server unresponsiveness.
verification_method: Static analysis of vulnerable code path, configuration review, attack vector validation through code inspection showing missing upper bound check when maxLimit is undefined
evidence_produced_by_run: Vulnerable code path identified in src/RestQuery.js:217-221 - limit passed without validation
maxLimit defaults to undefined in src/Options/Definitions.js:407-410
ClassesRouter.js:25 validation bypass confirmed when maxLimit=undefined
Attack vector validated: GET request with limit=1000000 to public classes requires no authentication (PR:N)
Expected impact: 1M+ record query consumes 1-2GB RAM, causes server unresponsiveness

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
